commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh But also you shall not cut off your kindness from my house forever: no, not when the LORD has cut off the enemies of David every one from the face of the earth. It is a curse upon himself, if he should not be faithful to his promise. That is, revenge it upon David's enemies, and upon me, if I shall fail of my word given to him. (Challoner) The Hebrew and several Latin manuscripts stop at earth; and what follows, is not found in some Greek and Latin editions. (Calmet) Enemies. May God punish David's enemies, and me among the rest. (Menochius).

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Require it: That is, revenge it upon David's enemies, and upon me, if I should fail of my word given to him.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: May he take away Jonathan: It is a curse upon himself, if he should not be faithful to his promise.-- Ibid.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
